title: Serious adverse event: late neurotropic disease associated with yellow fever vaccine.
ehr_evidence: None. The study is a single case report.
summary: A case report describing a healthy infant who developed late neurotropic disease, including strabismus and cervical muscle issues, 20 days after receiving the yellow fever vaccine.
key_findings: The patient developed neurological symptoms following yellow fever vaccination.
Diagnosis was confirmed by positive IgM in the liquor.
captured_features: Clinical symptoms (fever, chills, nausea, vomiting, strabismus, cervical muscle issues)
limitations: Not an EWAS or large-scale study
